result: {"base_sha":"e863ef073698b27519ccda07e8907c053d4cc7df","findings":[{"title":"Dial observer settlement is emitted outside the epoch mutex, so the next epoch's pending event can precede the prior epoch's settlement in the trace","evidence":"crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:815 — resize_settled_locked calls resize_settlement_notify.notify_waiters() while the epoch mutex is still held, but resize_settled emits DialObservationEvent::Settlement only after drop(state) at crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:779-791. A tuner blocked in wait_for_resize_settlement (dial.rs:822-832, awaited at dial.rs:1136 and crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1131) can therefore wake, claim epoch N+1 in apply_sample_input, and emit its Sample/Pending events (dial.rs:943-966) before the settling task reaches its Settlement emit. Field values are captured under the mutex so contents stay exact; only cross-event ordering is unsynchronized.","predicted_failure":"If the settling task is preempted between drop(state) and observer.emit while the tuner completes a full notify-wake→sample round-trip, the session-phase JSONL records dial_pending for epoch N+1 before dial_settlement for epoch N. A hardware-review consumer reading records in file order sees a proposal opened while the prior epoch appears unsettled, and the ordered dial_event_semantics role-parity comparison in dial_observer_is_inert_and_reports_peak_separately_from_final (crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:6661,6692) could flake if the inversion strikes one layout only. The window is a few instructions against a multi-hop round-trip (production adds a full 250ms tuner tick), so the probability is vanishing; no policy, membership, wire, or transfer-result behavior is affected.","severity":"LOW","better_approach":"Make the record order explicit instead of schedule-dependent: move notify_waiters() out of resize_settled_locked and fire it in resize_settled after the observer emit (unconditionally whenever settled — the check-before-await loop in wait_for_resize_settlement already tolerates late notification), so no tuner can claim the next epoch before the Settlement record exists; alternatively stamp observer events with a monotonic sequence number taken under the epoch mutex so consumers have an authoritative order."}],"guard_confirmed":true,"reviewed_sha":"436e1bb5f29ca9ea1dece6eb2c5656a63bce7564","verdict":"findings"}

tool_input.command: rtk git status && rtk git log --oneline -5 && git rev-parse HEAD
tool_input.description: Show worktree status and recent commits
Show diffstat of the reviewed change

findings.title: Dial observer settlement is emitted outside the epoch mutex, so the next epoch's pending event can precede the prior epoch's settlement in the trace
findings.evidence: crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:815 — resize_settled_locked calls resize_settlement_notify.notify_waiters() while the epoch mutex is still held, but resize_settled emits DialObservationEvent::Settlement only after drop(state) at crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:779-791. A tuner blocked in wait_for_resize_settlement (dial.rs:822-832, awaited at dial.rs:1136 and crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1131) can therefore wake, claim epoch N+1 in apply_sample_input, and emit its Sample/Pending events (dial.rs:943-966) before the settling task reaches its Settlement emit. Field values are captured under the mutex so contents stay exact; only cross-event ordering is unsynchronized.
findings.predicted_failure: If the settling task is preempted between drop(state) and observer.emit while the tuner completes a full notify-wake→sample round-trip, the session-phase JSONL records dial_pending for epoch N+1 before dial_settlement for epoch N. A hardware-review consumer reading records in file order sees a proposal opened while the prior epoch appears unsettled, and the ordered dial_event_semantics role-parity comparison in dial_observer_is_inert_and_reports_peak_separately_from_final (crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:6661,6692) could flake if the inversion strikes one layout only. The window is a few instructions against a multi-hop round-trip (production adds a full 250ms tuner tick), so the probability is vanishing; no policy, membership, wire, or transfer-result behavior is affected.
findings.better_approach: Make the record order explicit instead of schedule-dependent: move notify_waiters() out of resize_settled_locked and fire it in resize_settled after the observer emit (unconditionally whenever settled — the check-before-await loop in wait_for_resize_settlement already tolerates late notification), so no tuner can claim the next epoch before the Settlement record exists; alternatively stamp observer events with a monotonic sequence number taken under the epoch mutex so consumers have an authoritative order.
